svg.title: FDAI system architecture
svg.desc: Azure changes, telemetry, operator requests, and scheduled probes enter Event Hubs through its Kafka endpoint on port 9093. The FDAI control plane ingests each event, selects a trust tier, verifies the decision, applies the risk gate, and either executes, requests approval, or holds. Outcomes are delivered through remediation pull requests and recorded in PostgreSQL for the read-only console.
svg.text: Signals enter a governed control plane and leave through approval-aware delivery paths.

g.aria-label: Operational signals
1. Headless FDAI control plane
Human and delivery surfaces
Governed control flow
5. Rule catalog
4. Human channel
2. Action delivery
3. Operator console
Azure resource changes. Resource discovery and change notifications
Metrics, logs, and traces. Azure Monitor operational evidence
Operator request. Operator request
Scheduled probe. Scheduled probe
Event Hubs / Kafka :9093. Azure-managed event bus consumed only through the Kafka wire endpoint
Event ingest. Validate, normalize, deduplicate, and correlate
Trust router. Select the lowest competent T0, T1, or T2 tier
T0 / T1 / T2 decision. Deterministic rules, evidence reuse, or grounded reasoning
Verification and safety check. Verify evidence and apply the autonomy ceiling
Privileged executor. Idempotent action with lock, stop condition, and rollback
Held for review. No change is applied when evidence or policy is insufficient
Rollback path. Restore or move state forward after a failed action
PostgreSQL and pgvector. Append-only audit, state, evidence, and context
Rule and action catalog. Rule and action catalog
Human approval in ChatOps. Human approval in ChatOps
Remediation pull request. Remediation pull request
Read-only console. Read-only console
